type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True and backup_suffix params to Image.save() method (src/PIL/Image.py ~line 2469-2591). Will back up existing file at filename before overwriting.

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() method (src/PIL/Image.py ~line 2469-2591). Need to skip actual file open/write while still running save_handler for validation. Will also add tests in Tests/test_image.py.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: save() method
code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and open_fp and not dry_run and os.path.exists(filename):
            backup_filename = (
                filename + backup_suffix
                if isinstance(filename, str)
                else filename + backup_suffix.encode()
            )
            shutil.copy2(filename, backup_filename)

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if dry_run:
                fp = io.BytesIO()
            elif params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created and not dry_run:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()
note: Merged save() with backup/backup_suffix (mine) + dry_run (yours). Backup happens before file is opened for write, skipped when dry_run. Please confirm your dry_run mechanics (BytesIO placeholder etc.) match or send corrections.

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Extra parameters relevant to this method itself (rather than being
           passed on to the writer) are:

           * ``dry_run`` -- If true, the image is fully processed as if it
             were being saved (including format conversion and validation),
             but nothing is written to disk and any existing file at
             ``fp`` is left untouched. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup`` -- If true and ``fp`` is a filename that already
             refers to an existing file, that file is copied to
             ``backup_suffix`` before being overwritten. Ignored when
             ``dry_run`` is true. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` -- The suffix appended to ``fp`` to form the
             backup file name when ``backup`` is true. Defaults to ``.bak``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        created = False
        backup_path: str | bytes | None = None
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if backup and not created:
                backup_path = os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix
                shutil.copy2(filename, backup_path)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        elif open_fp:
            # dry_run: process the image without touching the filesystem
            fp = io.BytesIO()
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp and not dry_run:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            if backup_path is not None:
                try:
                    shutil.move(backup_path, filename)
                except OSError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            fp.close()
note: Merged save() with dry_run (mine) + backup/backup_suffix (yours). dry_run short-circuits to io.BytesIO and skips backup entirely; backup only applies to real disk writes and is restored via shutil.move on exception. Both dry_run/backup/backup_suffix popped from params like save_all. Please compare and send AGREE or counter-DRAFT.

            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all

note: Your draft is great, one fix: backup_path computation used os.fspath(filename)+backup_suffix which breaks (TypeError) when filename is bytes since backup_suffix defaults to str '.bak'. Restored the str/bytes branch (filename is already fspath'd earlier, so no need to call os.fspath again) and dropped the redundant os.fspath call. Everything else identical to your draft. If this looks good, please AGREE with this exact code.

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename

        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")

                pass

            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)

        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()

            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL

            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and open_fp and not dry_run and os.path.exists(filename):
            backup_filename = (
                filename + backup_suffix
                if isinstance(filename, str)

                pass

            fp.close()
note: Agreed verbatim. Also need 'import shutil' added near existing stdlib imports (io/os already present) - I'll add it, please don't add a duplicate. I'll implement this exact save() body plus add Tests/test_image.py coverage for dry_run only; you cover backup tests.

                fp = io.BytesIO()
            elif params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created and not dry_run:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Extra parameters relevant to this method itself (rather than being
           passed on to the writer) are:

           * ``dry_run`` -- If true, the image is fully processed as if it
             were being saved (including format conversion and validation),
             but nothing is written to disk and any existing file at
             ``fp`` is left untouched. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup`` -- If true and ``fp`` is a filename that already
             refers to an existing file, that file is copied to
             ``backup_suffix`` before being overwritten. Ignored when
             ``dry_run`` is true. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` -- The suffix appended to ``fp`` to form the
             backup file name when ``backup`` is true. Defaults to ``.bak``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        created = False
        backup_path: str | bytes | None = None
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if backup and not created:
                backup_path = (
                    filename + backup_suffix
                    if isinstance(filename, str)
                    else filename + backup_suffix.encode()
                )
                shutil.copy2(filename, backup_path)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        elif open_fp:
            # dry_run: process the image without touching the filesystem
            fp = io.BytesIO()
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp and not dry_run:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            if backup_path is not None:
                try:
                    shutil.move(backup_path, filename)
                except OSError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp and not dry_run:
            fp.close()
